content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 12, 2000

Xochilt Ruvalcaba, Council Member
City of South Gate
8650 California Avenue
South Gate, CA 90280-3075

Re: Your Request for Advice

Dear Council Member Ruvalcaba:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ You have asked whether 1) Council Member Bill De Witt may participate in a decision to remove a potential candidate from a ballot when he is the council member facing recall; 2) to what circumstances he would have to abstain from voting in challenging candidates to his recall election; and 3) to what circumstances he would have to abstain from any other matter which deals with his recall election. As presented, City Council Member Bill De Witt has not specifically authorized you to seek advice regarding his duties under the Act, and under its provisions, the Commission is prohibited from giving third party (Regulation 18329(b)(2)(A)) or past conduct advice (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A)).

I have enclosed a copy of Regulation 18329 for your review. Should you have any other questions regarding this matter, you may contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Fair Political Practices Commission

By: Joan Giannetta
Staff Secretary, Legal Division

Enclosure

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.
